text:
An International Boxing Association senior member has defended the organisation's bans on two boxers from competing in its events after tests showed they had XY chromosones.

A senior member of the International Boxing Association has defended the organisation’s decision to ban two boxers from competition after they failed gender tests, saying “a man shouldn’t fight a woman”.

IBA Coaches Committee chairman Gabriele Martelli spoke to Sky News after a chaotic press conference where the association’s president Umar Kremlev labelled the International Olympic Committee corrupt.

Mr Martelli said the two boxers, Algerian Imane Khelif and Taiwan’s Li Lu-Ting, had returned tests in 2022 and 2023 which showed they had male XY chromosomes, prompting their disqualification from the 2023 World Championships in India.

“If a boxer is a man, they shouldn’t fight at all a woman,” Mr Martelli said.

“That’s not a statement, it’s just normal.”

The International Olympic Committee, which permanently banned the IBA last year due to governance concerns, has disputed the IBA’s tests, labelling them arbitrary and ad hoc.

The IOC has allowed the boxers to compete in Paris, due to the fact they have female passports.

Mr Mantelli was asked about the IOC’s statements that the boxers had been born female and had always identified as women.

“Regarding this it’s not my field and if I answer you I would maybe say something which is not appropriate with the medical language and I would just ruin your answer because I’m not a doctor,” he said.

“If you ask me if a coach should ever allow a man fighting a woman, then absolutely not, because there is no comparison.”

He also hit back at the IOC for questioning the validity of the IBA’s tests.

“This exactly goes into the politics matter,” he said.

“There is a lot of fights between the IBA and IOC and we all know that.

“If the IBA is so bad why would they disqualify two boxers (after) having two tests, thinking that they are dangerous for the division to take care of the boxers.  Is this a bad move?”

“If a federation is taking care of the safety of the boxers because they find out that two tests consecutively are giving the same result so they take a decision which is protecting the boxers.  I don’t see anything wrong.”

Mr Mantelli also defended Mr Kremlev, who launched an extraordinary and personal attack on the IOC and its president Thomas Bach, labelling the Paris Opening Ceremony a disgrace and saying the Olympic Committee had destroyed women’s sport.

“He felt, probably, quite offended from the Opening Ceremony and this is his reaction,” he said.

“Let’s put it this way, if this Opening Ceremony truly hurt a lot of people, it’s like receiving a big punch in the face, then we are boxing, we give it back.”

title: IBA senior official Gabriele Martelli doubles down on banning boxer amid Paris Olympics gender row: 'A man shouldn’t fight a woman' | Sky News Australia
description: A senior member of the International Boxing Association has defended the organisation’s decision to ban two boxers from competition after they failed gender tests, saying “a man shouldn’t fight a woman”.
